questions.question: A circle has center at (3, −2) and passes through the point (7, 1). Find the equation of the circle in standard form.
questions.explanation: Distance from center (3, −2) to point (7, 1) is r = √[(7−3)² + (1−(−2))²] = √[16 + 9] = 5. Standard form is (x − h)² + (y − k)² = r².

questions.question: A sector of a circle with radius 12 has a central angle of 75°. What is the area of the sector?
The circle x² + y² − 6x + 8y − 11 = 0 is reflected across the y-axis. Write the equation of the reflected circle in standard form.
questions.explanation: Complete the square: (x−3)² + (y+4)² = 36, center (3, −4), radius 6. Reflection across y-axis gives center (−3, −4). Equation: (x+3)² + (y+4)² = 36.

questions.question: A tangent line to the circle (x − 2)² + (y − 3)² = 13 passes through the point (5, 7) on the circle. What is the slope of this tangent line?
questions.explanation: The radius to point (5, 7) has slope (7−3)/(5−2) = 4/3. The tangent is perpendicular to the radius, so its slope is −3/4 × (−1) = −3/4. Actually: m_radius = 4/3, so m_tangent = −3/4. Wait—check: slope from center (2,3) to (5,7) is 4/3, tangent slope is −3/4. Recalculate: −1/(4/3) = −3/4. But verify: we need −3/4, not −2/3. Correcting: tangent slope = −3/4.

questions.question: An arc of a circle with radius 8 has arc length 6π. Find the central angle in degrees.
Two circles have equations x² + y² = 25 and (x − 6)² + y² = 49. At how many points do these circles intersect?
questions.explanation: Circle 1: center (0, 0), radius 5. Circle 2: center (6, 0), radius 7. Distance between centers = 6. Since 6 = 7 − 5, the circles are internally tangent, touching at exactly one point.
